scope: host-side build script + command builder hook only; no contract flip, no routing, no chart change

validation_rules_enforced: services must contain at least one entry

commits_landed_to_canonical_main.summary: feat(gmd-app): first-party source-build PoC for frontend (script + command builder, not yet routed)

what_remains_before_contract_flip_to_sou: Build the remaining 10 default first-party services in addition to frontend: adservice, cartservice (nested context), checkoutservice, currencyservice, emailservice, loadgenerator, paymentservice, productcatalogservice, recommendationservice, shippingservice. shoppingassistantservice remains optional (chart default `create: false`).

Decide whether the in-cluster cluster-builder pod is required for gmd-app builds; if yes, mirror per-language base images that gmd-app Dockerfiles depend on (golang:1.26.1-alpine, gcr.io/distroless/static, mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/sdk:10.0.100-noble, mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/runtime-deps:10.0.0-noble-chiseled, plus the Java/Node/Python bases of the other services).

Wire the gmd-app build dispatch in scripts/amof/api/routers/release.py release_lifecycle_action() so action='build' for ecosystem='gmd' picks build_gmd_app_lifecycle_build_command instead of build_lifecycle_build_command.

Decide chart integration approach (Option A: edit chart values.yaml in place; Option B: per-env helm.parameters in applicationset.yaml for dev only; Option C: per-env values files). Per UP8-source-boundary audit, Option B is recommended for first proof.
Apply the chosen chart integration: flip images.repository -> host.k3d.internal:5000/gmd and images.tag -> the gmd-app source-build tag for the targeted environment(s).

Flip _ecosystem_build_contract('gmd') from mirror_upstream to source_build with produces / source_repos / build_command metadata. This unhides Build in the UI and admits the API build POST. Update the lifecycle contract test accordingly.
Run the full gmd lifecycle build via the live API and verify that all targeted gmd-app images land in the local registry and that the live gmd-dev environment pulls them via the chart.

what_to_implement: Extend scripts/build-gmd-app.sh to support a second small service (e.g. emailservice or productcatalogservice) so the multi-service shape gets exercised before the full set lands.
Update _GMD_APP_BUILD_SUPPORTED_SERVICES in command_builder.py and the command builder validation tests.
Run the script for both services on host and verify both images appear in localhost:5000/gmd/<service>:<tag>.
Still no contract flip, no routing, no chart change.
